content: I'm planning to convert my guest bedroom into a proper home office. Working from home has become more common at SpaceX, and I need a dedicated space away from the living room chaos.
That's a great idea! A dedicated home office can really improve productivity. What are you thinking for the layout and design?
I want to keep it minimal but functional. I've been learning rust programming in my free time and need space for dual monitors and my mechanical keyboard collection. The room is about 12x14 feet.
That's a good size for a home office. With your interest in programming, have you considered ergonomic furniture? A good chair and desk setup can make a big difference during long coding sessions.
Absolutely. I'm looking at standing desks since I sit so much at work already. Speaking of work, we just got approval for a new satellite launch next month, so things are pretty hectic at the office.
Congratulations on the launch approval! That's exciting. For your home office, what's your budget looking like? Standing desks can range quite a bit in price depending on features.
I'm hoping to keep it under $3,000 total for everything - desk, chair, shelving, and maybe some soundproofing since my fiancé sometimes has early conference calls in the next room.

$3,000 is a reasonable budget for quality office furniture. Since you mentioned your fiancé, are you still planning the wedding for next year? The home office timing might work well with any pre-wedding projects you have going on.
Yes, the wedding is set for June 15th next year. We're doing a lot of DIY stuff - I'm handling the calligraphy for the invitations and my fiancé is knitting some decor items. It's keeping us busy!

That sounds beautiful! Handmade elements make weddings so special. For your office soundproofing, have you considered acoustic panels? They come in various styles and could complement your minimalist aesthetic.
I like that idea. I've also been getting into origami lately - maybe I could make some decorative acoustic panels myself. I enjoy crafting when I'm not cooking Thai food or working on my vintage car.

DIY origami acoustic panels would be unique and personal! Your creative hobbies seem to balance out your technical work nicely. Would you like some recommendations for standing desks that would fit your space and budget?

That would be great, thanks. I'm also thinking about adding some plants - I've read they can improve air quality and reduce stress. Maybe some low-maintenance ones since I tend to forget watering when I'm deep in a rust project.

Snake plants and ZZ plants are practically indestructible and perfect for busy people. I'll send over some standing desk recommendations along with some plant suggestions that would work well in your new office space.
